name: Makai Lemon
headline: Biletnikoff-winning rookie buried behind Smith and Goedert
body:
Philadelphia traded up in the first round for Lemon, a 5-foot-11 USC product who won the Biletnikoff Award and graded out as one of college football's most efficient perimeter receivers. The pedigree is real: just three drops on roughly 175 targets over his final two seasons, with the lateral quickness and route polish to win from the slot or outside. The problem is opportunity. DeVonta Smith and Dallas Goedert already soak up the meaningful target share, and a hamstring issue that cost him practice time only complicates a rookie's path to early reps.

Projections of 58 catches for 821 yards and nearly five scores reflect that target-competition ceiling, landing him around a Round 8 redraft cost and roughly WR35 by ADP. That makes him a stash, not a starter, in 2026. The investment pays off after 2027, when Goedert could depart and vacate the volume Lemon needs to break out.
